prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: And lo, on the third quarter’s turning, the Royal Treasurer spake of "ARR," a silver goblet that ever refills itself, yet remains maddeningly empty to the King’s touch. Many knights of the "Sales Crusade" did pledge fealty to a most elusive relic called "Pipeline," a serpent of promises that grows two heads for every one that is severed. The Court Scribe, called CFO, did proclaim a great "Net Revenue Retention" of 120%, a number which surely vexes the angels, for how can one retain more than was ever given? Verily, the realm boasts of ten thousand "seats," yet I have searched the castle high and low and found not a single physical throne.
